sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 13

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 13:1
items.html:
(to the question) Bechor Notel Pi Shnayim [the firstborn takes a double portion]
See Answer 11, where it is explained that in the Bi'ah Kadma'a [first coming], made in the one above the upper one, when the MAN [Mayin Nukvin] rose from the Klipot [shells], no form had yet been made in them; see there. However, since they cleave to the AHP of the upper one — see Answer 69 — the Zivug [coupling] that came out there in the one above the upper one, for the sake of the upper one, came out also upon the added MAN of the lower one that cleaves to the upper one. Therefore it is discerned that in this Zivug there are two levels of ten Sefirot: the first is the level of ten Sefirot that came out upon the discernment of the MAN of the upper one itself; the second is the level of ten Sefirot that came out upon the added MAN of the lower one, which cleaves to the MAN of the upper one at the time of the Zivug. But the lower one cannot yet enjoy or clothe anything of the level of ten Sefirot that came out upon its own MAN, since it has no form yet, not having received even the discernment of Ibur [conception]. Therefore the upper one takes the lower one's portion into itself as a deposit, so long as the lower one is not fit to receive it. Thus the upper one has taken a double portion from this Zivug, for it took two parts: its own part and the part of the lower one. This is the secret of "the firstborn takes a double portion." As to why the upper one is called the firstborn, see Answer 15 (p. 743, Item 12; and p. 803, Item 101).
